title: Charmed Life - Chapter 8 (Fragment 2)
text:
She snapped her fingers over the bowl. The ingredients caught fire, all by themselves, and burned with small blue flames. “It’s working!” Gwendolen said excitedly. She snatched up a twist of newspaper from beside her and carefully untwisted it. “Now for a pinch of dragons’ blood.” She took a pinch of the dark brown powder and sprinkled it on the flames. There was a fizzing, and a thick smell of burning. Then the flames leaped up, a foot high, blazing a furious green and purple, coloring the whole room with dancing light.

Gwendolen’s face glowed in the green and purple. She rocked on her heels, chanting, chanting strings of things Cat could not understand. Then, still chanting, she leaned over and touched the spider. The spider grew. And grew. And grew more. It grew into a five-foot monster—a greasy roundness with two little eyes on the front, hanging like a hammock amid eight bent and jointed furry legs. Gwendolen pointed. The door of her room sprang open of its own accord—which made her smile exultantly—and the huge spider went silently creeping towards it, swaying on its hairy legs. It squeezed its legs inward to get through the door, and crept onward, down the passage beyond.

Gwendolen touched the other creatures, one by one. The earwigs lumbered up and off, like shiny horned cows, bright brown and glistening. The frogs rose up, as big as men, and walked flap, flop on their enormous feet, with their arms trailing like gorillas. Their mottled skin quivered, and little holes in it kept opening and shutting. The puffy place under their chins made gulping movements. The black beetle crawled on branched legs, such a big black slab that it could barely get through the door. Cat could see it, and all the others, going in a slow, silent procession down the grass-green glowing corridor.

“Where are they going?” he whispered.

Gwendolen chuckled. “I’m sending them to the dining room, of course. I don’t think the guests will want much supper.”

She took up a bone next, and knocked each end of it sharply on the floor. As soon as she let go of it, it floated up into the air. There was a soft clattering, and more bones came out of nowhere to join it. The green and purple flames roared and rasped. A skull arrived last of all, and a complete skeleton was dangling there in front of the flames. Gwendolen smiled with satisfaction and took up another bone.

But bones when they are bewitched have a way of remembering who they were. The dangling skeleton sighed, in a hollow, singing voice, “Poor Sarah Jane. I’m poor Sarah Jane. Let me rest.”

Gwendolen waved it impatiently towards the door. It went dangling off, still sighing, and a second skeleton dangled after it, sighing, “Bob the gardener’s boy. I din’t mean to do it.” They were followed by three more, each one singing softly and desolately of who it had been, and all five went slowly dangling after the black beetle. “Sarah Jane,” Cat heard from the corridor. “I din’t mean to.” “I was Duke of Buckingham once.”

Gwendolen took no notice of them and turned to the earthworm. It grew too. It grew into a massive pink thing as big as a sea serpent. Loops of it rose and fell and writhed all over the room. Cat was nearly sick. Its bare pink flesh had hairs on it like a pig’s bristles. There were rings on it like the wrinkles around his own knuckles. Its great sightless front turned blindly this way and that until Gwendolen pointed to the door. Then it set off slowly after the skeletons, length after length of bare pink loops.

vocabulary.definition: Affected by magic or enchanted.
vocabulary.example: But bones when they are bewitched have a way of remembering who they were.

vocabulary.definition: Burning very brightly and strongly.
vocabulary.example: Then the flames leaped up, a foot high, blazing a furious green and purple.

vocabulary.definition: Short, stiff hairs on an animal or plant.
vocabulary.example: Its bare pink flesh had hairs on it like a pig’s bristles.

vocabulary.definition: When components ignite or start burning by themselves.
vocabulary.example: The ingredients caught fire, all by themselves, and burned with small blue flames.

vocabulary.definition: To repeat or sing a phrase or song continuously and rhythmically.
vocabulary.example: She rocked on her heels, chanting, chanting strings of things Cat could not understand.

vocabulary.definition: A continuous rattling sound as of hard objects falling or striking each other.
vocabulary.example: There was a soft clattering, and more bones came out of nowhere to join it.

vocabulary.definition: Moving slowly and quietly, especially to avoid being noticed.
vocabulary.example: The huge spider went silently creeping towards it.

vocabulary.definition: Hanged or swung loosely.
vocabulary.example: A complete skeleton was dangling there in front of the flames.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows sadness and loneliness.
vocabulary.example: Each one singing softly and desolately of who it had been.

vocabulary.definition: Showing or feeling great happiness or triumph.
vocabulary.example: Which made her smile exultantly.

vocabulary.definition: Making a hissing or bubbling sound as gas escapes.
vocabulary.example: There was a fizzing, and a thick smell of burning.

vocabulary.definition: Extremely intense or powerful; full of anger or intensity.
vocabulary.example: A foot high, blazing a furious green and purple.

vocabulary.definition: Shining with a soft and diffused light.
vocabulary.example: Bright brown and glistening.

vocabulary.definition: The things that are used to make a dish or mixture.
vocabulary.example: The ingredients caught fire, all by themselves.

vocabulary.definition: To move in a slow, heavy, awkward way.
vocabulary.example: The earwigs lumbered up and off, like shiny horned cows.

vocabulary.definition: Having spots or patches of different colours or shades.
vocabulary.example: Their mottled skin quivered.

vocabulary.definition: A small amount of a powdered substance, taken between the fingers.
vocabulary.example: Now for a pinch of dragons’ blood.

vocabulary.definition: Slightly swollen or rounded in appearance.
vocabulary.example: The puffy place under their chins made gulping movements.

vocabulary.definition: To shake slightly and rapidly, especially because of being cold, frightened, or emotional.
vocabulary.example: Their mottled skin quivered.

vocabulary.definition: To emit a long deep breath expressing sadness or relief.
vocabulary.example: Still sighing, and a second skeleton dangled after it, sighing.

vocabulary.definition: To make a sudden sharp sound, or to move something quickly with a sudden sharp sound.
vocabulary.example: She snapped her fingers over the bowl.

vocabulary.definition: To quickly take something in your hand or arms.
vocabulary.example: She snatched up a twist of newspaper from beside her and carefully untwisted it.

vocabulary.definition: To scatter small drops or particles of a substance over something.
vocabulary.example: She sprinkled it on the flames.

vocabulary.definition: To press something tightly together.
vocabulary.example: It squeezed its legs inward to get through the door.

vocabulary.definition: To make twisting, squirming movements or contortions of the body.
vocabulary.example: Loops of it rose and fell and writhed all over the room.
